mnemonics: S.A.D. Time Series: Remember the three components - Seasonal (predictable patterns), Autocorrelation (data points are related to their own past values), and De-trending (removing the trend to focus on other components).
keyPoints: A time series is a sequence of data points measured at successive points in time.
The main goal is to decompose the series into its Trend, Seasonal, and Residual components.
Testing often involves checking for stationarity (constant mean and variance over time) before applying models like ARIMA.
commonMistakes: Using a model that assumes stationarity on a non-stationary time series.
Overfitting the model by including too many parameters.
Ignoring residual analysis and not checking if the model's errors are random (white noise).
